records: one owned finite strictly-increasing sample type covers construction, value/derivative evaluation, Taylor coefficients, and arbitrary-spacing integration without callbacks
cubic B-spline construction has boundary and knot policy distinct from the existing checked B-spline representation
existing checked B-spline methods own knot, coefficient, and derivative contracts; a raw-array duplicate would weaken the API
callback-bearing adaptive integration duplicates established QUADPACK safe options and needs no second callback design
ODE callback and continuation lifecycle review is a separate bounded task
residual-only session intentionally avoids unreviewed callback aliasing and matrix-layout contracts
